response:
### 1. Golden Rectangle Calculations

Given that the width of the golden rectangle is 2 meters and the ratio of length to width is the golden ratio $ \phi = \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} $, we can determine the length of the rectangle as follows:

$$
\text{length} = \phi \times \text{width} = \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} \times 2 = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

#### Area of the Rectangle

$$
\text{area} = \text{length} \times \text{width} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2 + 2\sqrt{5} \text{ square meters}
$$

#### Length of the Diagonal

Using the Pythagorean theorem:

$$
\text{diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{length}^2 + \text{width}^2}
$$

$$
\text{length}^2 = (1 + \sqrt{5})^2 = 1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5 = 6 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$
$$
\text{width}^2 = 2^2 = 4
$$
$$
\text{diagonal} = \sqrt{(6 + 2\sqrt{5}) + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}} \text{ meters}
$$

---

### 2. Inscribed Circle and Area Calculation

#### Radius of the Inscribed Circle

The diameter of the circle is equal to the width of the rectangle since the circle is inscribed within it. Therefore:

$$
\text{diameter} = 2 \text{ meters}
$$
$$
\text{radius} = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

#### Area of the Region Inside the Rectangle but Outside the Circle

$$
\text{area of rectangle} = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$
$$
\text{area of circle} = \pi r^2 = \pi (1)^2 = \pi
$$
$$
\text{area of region} = \text{area of rectangle} - \text{area of circle} = (2 + 2\sqrt{5}) - \pi
